raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "AB 404 will require the California Department of Food and Agriculture to evaluate duplicative reporting requirements that unnecessarily burden organic farmers.",
    "AB 405 will require the California Department of Public Health to move from a paper-based registration and payment process to an online system for organic businesses.",
    "AB 406 will allow organic farmers whose management practices contribute to better land quality and result in greenhouse gas reductions to apply for grants from the Healthy Soils Program and California Farmland Conservancy Program.",
    "Organic farming provides healthier food and improves local ecosystems.",
    "Organic food sales in California more than doubled between 2020 and 2021.",
    "The organic farming industry has grown to be a $53 billion industry over the last 20 years.",
    "Organic food is grown and processed without synthetic fertilizers or pesticides, resulting in crops with higher levels of vitamins, nutrients, and antioxidants.",
    "Federal law requires certified organic farms to maintain or improve soil health, which reduces the water needed for crops and stores more carbon in the soil."
  ]
}
```
